fields.description:
## Context

Parallel agent sessions race to land changes on the Fast-Forward-Only `main` (ADR-0040).
The loser of each race must manually rebase-to-tip and retry, and with a large feature
**stack** this update-and-retry causes many CI re-runs on non-conflicting changes — burning
**wall-clock** (GitHub Actions minutes are free for this OSS repo, but concurrency is capped)
and **agent tokens/attention** (sessions hand-roll the rebase + poll for submittability).

This epic builds the **auto-lander**: agents set an opt-in intent and walk away; changes land
autonomously (or are handed back with a single actionable outcome), while the strong two-vote
gate is never weakened.

> **Terminology.** "stack" (the agent's view of a related series) and "chain" (a Gerrit
> relation chain) are used synonymously throughout this epic and its children.

**Separation-of-concerns scope (the load-bearing decision):** landing/merge-queueing is
**platform-specific and maintainer-owned**, NOT part of rebar's platform-agnostic
ticket/workflow/gate/reconcile mission. So everything here is **THIS project's infrastructure**
(Gerrit + GitHub Actions), living in `infra/` + a project CLI/script — it MAY `import rebar` for
ticket ops (e.g. close a ticket on merge) but adds **no Gerrit-specific landing logic to
`src/rebar` core**. The platform-agnostic generalization is deliberately deferred to a separate
`idea` (the landing provider-seam, `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`).

**Design settled in the brainstorm (all grounded in OSS prior art):**
- **Phase 1 = serial lander (this epic's committed scope).** Land the front `Autosubmit`
  change/chain: rebase to tip → await fresh Verified → ancestor-atomic submit.
- **Phase 2 = throughput, DEFERRED.** Cross-stack batching was evaluated by a design spike and
  **rejected** on this infra (the gerrit-to-platform bridge fires CI per-patchset → N runs not one;
  landing N off one run needs a GerriScary-class Verified direct-cast; wins only in rare bursts of
  ≥4 + a bisection tax + WIP-hook fragility). The recommended throughput approach — **bounded
  speculation** (speculate ≤ the GHA concurrency cap; each candidate a clean single-change-on-tip
  verified on its own tree) — is deferred to idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`, promoted only if the serial
  treadmill becomes a MEASURED pain.
- **Gate substrate stays:** FFO as the R5 backstop; strict `Verified` copyCondition
  (drops on rebase → CI re-runs); LLM-Review carries `TRIVIAL_REBASE` (ADR-0041) so a
  conflict-free rebase re-runs ONLY CI, not the LLM review.
- **`Autosubmit` opt-in label = a lease the agent hands the bot;** coexists with manual submit.
- **The stack is the atomic unit for landing AND failure:** never partial-land, never evict a
  change from *within* a stack; on failure the **whole stack is handed back to its owning agent**
  to remediate (Goal-2 exception: agent is pulled in only when code must change).
- **Agent contract = one command → one typed outcome** (`merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed |
  review_failed`); the bot is the single source of truth for the outcome. Delivered as project
  tooling, reading Gerrit primitives so agents never correlate votes/labels/status themselves.
  **Outcome provenance:** the bot *records* `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` in its own state (S3);
  `merged`/`review_failed` are *derived* from native Gerrit status / an `LLM-Review -1` by the
  land command (S4).

## Greenfield note

This epic is greenfield: the `Autosubmit` label, the autolander service + its `wipChain` and state dir, the `land`/`land-status` command + outcome enum, the outcome-record schema, and ADR-0042 are all NEW artifacts created by this epic's stories. Cross-story references to them are forward-references to sibling deliverables (see each story's `## Dependencies`), not references to existing code.

## Success Criteria

- An agent sets the landing intent and walks away; in the common (non-conflicting) case the
  change/stack lands with **zero further agent attention** and **no manual rebase-retry**.
- On any failure the agent receives **one typed, actionable outcome** — it never watches votes or
  correlates multiple Gerrit primitives, and the "both votes green but conflicts" trap is handled
  inside the tool, not by the agent.
- **Nothing lands whose to-be-merged tree wasn't CI-tested** (FFO + strict Verified preserved;
  two-vote gate never weakened — GerriScary posture audited).
- Redundant CI runs from the parallel-landing race are eliminated (each change/stack is CI'd only
  as needed by the serial lander). Throughput beyond serial is a deferred Phase-2 concern (bounded
  speculation, idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`), not a committed success criterion here.
- **No Gerrit-specific landing logic in `src/rebar` core** — rebar stays platform-agnostic.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Phase 1 — serial auto-lander (first deliverable)
- [ ] `Autosubmit` Gerrit label authored in `infra/gerrit/project.config` / pushed to
      `refs/meta/config`: **requester-votable ACL** (vote granted to group `Contributors`, so an
      agent sets the label under its OWN identity via the land command — the bot casts no votes;
      the grant is tightly scoped, not a bot-only or open grant), **non-gating** (informational;
      gating is redundant under FFO),
      **non-sticky** (empty copyCondition).
- [ ] Serial auto-lander service (project infra; deploys like the review-bot —
      `infra/compose/Dockerfile.*` + compose): for the front `Autosubmit`+submittable change/chain
      it rebases to the current tip (`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / `/rebase:chain`,
      `on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers) → awaits a fresh
      `Verified +1` → ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA. Single `wipChain` (serialized,
      one instance).
- [ ] **No gate-semantics change** — the serial lander reads existing `Verified` votes and casts
      NO votes (the bot never casts Verified in this epic's committed scope).
- [ ] Failure handling: CI-fail → remove `Autosubmit`, rely on the native `Verified -1` + run
      logs; rebase-conflict (`409`) → remove `Autosubmit` and record a conflict outcome. **Never
      partial-land; never evict a change from within a stack** — the whole stack is handed back to
      its owning agent.
- [ ] **Agent landing contract (the lead story):** a single **project** command that requests a
      land and returns ONE typed terminal outcome (`merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed |
      review_failed`) + distinct exit code; blocking (`--wait`) and single-shot status variants.
      The bot is the single source of truth for the outcome. The command lives in **project
      tooling** (repo CLI/script), NOT `rebar` core; it may `import rebar` for ticket ops.
- [ ] Observability: the bot logs to **its own service logs** (container stdout → existing
      CloudWatch monitoring), NOT `session_log` (which is for agentic sessions only).
- [ ] Docs: rewrite this repo's landing section (`CLAUDE.md` + `CONTRIBUTING.md`) to the new
      contract — "set `Autosubmit` / run the land command; act on the single typed outcome; do
      NOT watch votes or treat two green votes as 'it will land'." ADR-0042 (created by S6
      `broken-checkered-tiger`) records the auto-lander + agent contract.
- [ ] **Security-invariants artifact:** an ADR-0042 **Security invariants** section enumerating
      each invariant — non-gating `Autosubmit`; requester-votable (`Contributors`) vote ACL, tightly scoped; re-verify `is:submittable` on
      the exact tested SHA immediately before submit; two-vote gate unweakened — and, per
      invariant, the concrete check that proves it (a `grep` of `infra/gerrit/project.config`, a
      `GET /projects/rebar/access` diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot, and/or a test asserting the
      re-verify logic) (CVE-2025-1568 / GerriScary posture).

### Phase 2 — throughput (DEFERRED to idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`)
- [ ] No Phase-2 work is in this epic's committed scope. Cross-stack batching was evaluated and
      **rejected** by the design spike (stories S7-S9 archived); the throughput follow-on is
      **bounded speculation**, promoted from the idea only on a measured serial-treadmill pain.

## Explicitly OUT of scope (separate tickets)

- **Cross-stack batching** — evaluated by the Phase-2 design spike and **rejected** on this infra
  (per-patchset CI dispatch → N runs not one; a GerriScary-class `Verified` direct-cast; rare wins +
  bisection tax + WIP-hook fragility). S7-S9 archived; throughput deferred to **bounded speculation**
  (idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`) — the correctly-shaped follow-on, not "speculation ruled out."
- **Platform-agnostic landing provider-seam in `rebar` core** (Gerrit/GitHub/GitLab adapters +
  portable outcome enum) — deferred to a separate `idea` (`1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`; YAGNI until a
  second platform needs to land; consistent with rebar's provider-neutral identity/reconciler
  direction).
